codeReview.summary: The fix resolves the original blocking gap from `brainstorm-entry-modal-and-auth-flows`: the signed-in placeholder state is now replaced by a real library-first flow with authenticated CRUD routes, empty/populated library states, New Session/Reopen/Delete actions, clean workspace bootstrap for new sessions, deleted-session reopen rejection, and auth-change-keyed workspace gating so sign-out/sign-in transitions return to the library modal. I found one smaller follow-up on the frontend failure path: library action promises are intentionally discarded in the modal even though the hook rethrows after setting UI errors, which can surface uncaught promise rejections in the console when create/reopen/delete requests fail.

issues.description: The library action buttons fire `void onCreateSession()`, `void onReopenSession(...)`, and `void onDeleteSession(...)` at lines 229, 318, and 330, but the underlying hook still rethrows request failures after storing the user-facing error (`frontend/src/hooks/useBrainstormSessionLibrary.ts:226`, `:257`, `:286`). On any backend failure path this leaves the modal showing a friendly error while also producing an uncaught rejected promise in the console, so deleted-session reopen and other request failures are not completely clean from a validation/console-errors standpoint.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the fix transcript skeleton plus both the original failing diff (`8be1194`) and the fix diff (`7a22eaa`). The fix closes the prior blocking issue by replacing the signed-in placeholder with a real library-first CRUD flow and by keying workspace unlocks to auth-state changes; the only remaining issue I found is a non-blocking uncaught-promise path on failed library actions.
